text.title: Google Shopping Ads Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-shopping-ads methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Merchant Center feed health MUST be ≥80% complete (titles, GTIN, images, attributes) before launch.
rule.rationale: Below 80%, products are disapproved or under-served; spend wasted on the eligible minority.

rule.statement: Inventory MUST be split into ≥2 priority-tier campaigns (high / low or high / mid / clearance) with distinct ROAS targets.
rule.rationale: Single Shopping campaign drowns high-margin SKUs in low-margin volume; tier-split lets bid strategy optimize each.

rule.statement: Product groups MUST be partitioned by margin or category, not 'All products'. Single-bucket 'All products' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bid strategy needs partitioning to find ROAS lift per slice.

rule.statement: Negative keywords (broad / phrase / exact) MUST be applied on Shopping to exclude irrelevant queries (free / used / job).
rule.rationale: Shopping does not bid on keywords but DOES match queries; junk queries burn budget.

rule.statement: ROAS target MUST be set per priority tier (high tier ≥4, mid ≥2.5, clearance ≥1.5).
rule.rationale: Uniform ROAS target either starves high-margin or floods clearance.
